DMG: 40 Dmg Bonus: 142
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.

this is probally better than most twink wepons u can get unless u have well over 100k to spend on them. its not very good until level 20 but then u can kill recondites in 4 hits- and with doubble attack thats only 2 rounds, in which they will do what 50 damage? also get a good haste item and u will attack around once every 3 seconds. <a href='http://www.smileycentral.com/?partner=ZSzeb008_ZNxdm395YYUS' target='_blank'><img src='http://smileys.smileycentral.com/cat/7/7_5_128.gif' border=0></a>

▲ Page top
Any good?
QuoteReply
# Aug 07 2004 at 10:47 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770826864
View User Forum
26 posts
I am not certain what the formulas are. I have used the reaver for my low level twinks for about 2 years. What I found was this:
With a Wide Bladed Greatsword my toons had fewer hit points remaining when the mob died than with the Reaver. The same result with other 2 hs. The result vs dual wield varied. I used a Wurmy and Centi LS, a Defiance and Lamentation, etc. With some I had more hps left when the mob was dead, with others fewer hps. I did not do any of this scientifically, obviously. My beliefs are based on completely empirical (observed) data. The Reaver is great weapon.
I particularly like the resist stats, but I have several high end characters that resist stats are important to. I will mention that they use other weapons with even better damage/delay ratios. :=).
▲ Page top
I Got So Ripped Off!!!
QuoteReply
# Feb 22 2004 at 11:13 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591767080873
View User Forum
64 posts
When I rolled my Zerker I went shopping to twink him out. When I saw a weapon with a 100% damage delay ratio and no level restriction for ONLY 10Kpp I jumped on it. That was about a week ago and since then I have seen it auctioned many, many times for under 6K and once as low as 4k.

To add insult to injury, I have hardly been using the Reaver b/c the damage cap at low levels prevents it from hitting as hard as it should. Instead I've been using a Sword of the Crypt Keeper that I looted for FREE on an LDoN mission (has a 50HP lifetap proc that works at level 1).

Although I'm sure I'll be happy with this when I reach level 20 and the last damage cap is removed, I really wish I hadn't paid that much.

I jumped on this at 10K b/c I'm so used to buying weapons for my Monk - any Monk 2h'er w/ those stats would sell for much, much more.

Anyone on Tholuxe Paells wanna buy a "like new" Reaver for 10k?........lol
▲ Page top
RE: I Got So Ripped Off!!!
QuoteReply
# Sep 03 2004 at 2:47 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593362009150
144 posts
Sword of the Crypt Keeper was nerfed... it has a recommended lvl of 55 and it doesn't proc until lvl 30. So it definitely isn't better than the reaver. And as far as the cap... wait till 20... omfg you will be hurting people. My 23 zerker crits for around 110-120ish and he does it alot. Average hit is 60-70. Thats alot of dmg at 23. That doesn't include my frenzy which tears em up. all in all... this is a very nice wep... even if your capped... but have patience till your 20... it'll pay off.

▲ Page top
Reaver just got a whole lot better
QuoteReply
# Jan 06 2004 at 3:45 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591789574910
6 posts
Sony's removal of stamina in the form of battle fatigue (the main complaint against this weapon in the past due to its very high 25 weight) has made this a much better 2HS option. It has to be considered one of the top 3 or 4 purchasable 2HS weapons in the game without question. I have one on my 31 Shadow Knight, and I have never been outdamaged by any party member I've grouped with. I loved my reaver before but would occasionally have to have the cleric refresh my stamina in long, drawn-out battles. Now it's no longer a concern.
▲ Page top
RE: Reaver just got a whole lot better
QuoteReply
# Mar 30 2004 at 2:18 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593362983603
35 posts
I can name ya 3 other weapons that are easily better buys than this reaver. A Di'zok Katana, Sword of crypt keeper, and War marshall's bladed staff to name a few, let alone a Tae ew 2hs, Argent Protector , and Widebladed Greatsword. I use to own this weapon, along with all those others, and this reaver, especially due to the weight , was used not worth it, and used mebbe three times. I sold mine, and never even thought about using it to twink an alt. As far as the resists go, i can get that off of jewlery. I do not need a slow, sloppy 2hs for good resists. Even after the stamina change, i would have little use for this blade.

▲ Page top
Amazing Weapon
QuoteReply
# Jun 24 2003 at 1:59 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593362287474
4 posts
If you want a great 2hander for cheap, get this weapon. It has a higher dps than any weapon with less than a 1:1 ratio. Using the calculation of [2*(dmg+dmg bonus)/dly]*10*(1+ %haste--like 1.41)*[1+(player level+double attack skill level)/500], you will see that only weapons like Windblade and the like can match or surpass this weapon. Just picked one up for 7k on Quellious, couldn't believe the price. There were weapons costing three times as much that don't even come close to it. Yes there are weapons with better bonus stats on them but in the end the only thing that matters is dps.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 27 2003 at 9:42 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) This is definitely not a Warrior's first choice. The thing weighs 25 stones!!! Even with a high Stamina the 25 stones would slow a warrior down too soon in a fight.
RE: Amazing Weapon
QuoteReply
# Sep 01 2003 at 12:24 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1591750976102
281 posts
just because the weight is 25 stone doesnt make this a bad weapon. if your base STA is over 100 you will not slow down with attacks, in fact the only affect is you wouldnt be able to jump but i have still yet to meet a warrior who jumps while fighting.
▲ Page top
RE: Amazing Weapon
QuoteReply
# Sep 17 2003 at 10:02 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592343807431
Sage
3,128 posts
Warriors should avoid 2 handed weapons anyway they get better aggro hold using 2 low delay procing weapons with much more hits, and aggro from each hit. A pally could use this well especially since they get stamina replenish spells like invigor and have high channelling to cast it successfully in battle, and their stuns make up for aggo lost by high delay 2handed weapons (aggro lost from less hits per second).
▲ Page top
Reaver rocks
QuoteReply
# May 09 2003 at 7:22 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591788439512
11 posts
Last weapon was a Worn Shai`din Naginata, was a good weapon, but this Reaver is better. Don't know why other posting say the Reaver is nonmagical, rest assured, my Reaver is magical (patch anyone?). Am a 37 pally hitting in the 140's highest with it with 154 strength unbuffed. Saw it on Rodcet Nife at the cheapest for 3k, usually runs for 6k. With a Shaman haste spell on it this weapon becomes a 40/28 (30% haste) or 40/24 (40%haste). That is a killer weapon. Everyone says the Widebladed Greatsword is better, but consider: they both have the same ratio, 1 to 1, only difference is instead of resistances you get buffs to attributes with Greatsword and 25 AC with Greatsword. 25 AC is killer, but with my 40% haste on this weapon, I'll take the Reaver anyday. The Reaver weighs alot, though.....stamina problems.....

Oh and for those disillusioned souls who think they are going to be able to get the cash to buy an admittedly superior but incredibly, outlandishly expensive 45k Windblade, this weapon is the second best out there, or the Widebladed Greatsword (both 2nd best). And if you think you're gonna kill a mob to get the Windblade, bettter wait until you're level 63 because that's what you're gonna have to do.

Hifglar Stormblade
37 Paladin
▲ Page top
RE: Reaver rocks
QuoteReply
# Jul 09 2003 at 5:31 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592258339781
37 posts
At one point of time this wep was non magical. I also remember when they where dirt cheap because of it. I was actualy surprised that they went back to fix the flag. /shrug I might have to go get one for my war on SZ now
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 04 2003 at 11:18 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) /rant
RE: Reaver rocks
QuoteReply
# Jun 05 2003 at 6:31 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591787895048
104 posts
I dont believe you have ever played or even grped with a melee character in EQ after that amazingly misinformed post.

Lmao haste DOES NOT make you hit harder, it makes you hit more often.

However 50 percent haste does not cut 50 percent of the delay off your weapon. It works like this-
(weapon delay)/(1 + percent haste)

so a 40 delay weapon with 50 percent haste is

40/(1 + .50) = 26.6

but add in another 50 percent haste, and you only go down to

40/(1 +1) = 20

▲ Page top
RE: Reaver rocks
QuoteReply
# Mar 10 2013 at 1:38 PM Rating: Decent
Kaenneth
13 posts
he said DPS, not Damage.
▲ Page top
RE: Reaver rocks
QuoteReply
# Dec 14 2003 at 11:03 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593362009150
144 posts
wow... im gonna have to agree with you on this one... that was a dumb-*** post... my iq has been reduced from reading it, i shall go now and try to recover from it
▲ Page top
RE: Reaver rocks
QuoteReply
# Jun 24 2003 at 12:39 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
Not only is he wrong about the haste, every other piece of info he gives is wrong too.

Wurmslayer is 25 damage 40 delay
Haste on velium swiftblade is 36%

And the swiftblade/Wurmy combo IS a damned nice ranger setup, which can last you up to lvl 50 or so.
▲ Page top
RE: Reaver rocks
QuoteReply
# Dec 23 2003 at 6:43 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592758514208
47 posts
Not exactly sure how haste works but this how someone explained it to me 50% haste will not make this a 40/20 if it DID, having 100% haste would mean you were hitting non-stop making the delay ZERO.

Instead he said it works like this (simplified)

Divide 100 by HASTE, this is how many hits you would normally do but with haste in the same time you do 1 extra

Example. Haste 25%
100 / 25 = 4, meaning every time you would normally hit 4 times you would hit 5 instead. Making you 25% more efficent in combat.

Don't flame it you know this info is wrong by all means post, this is what I was told and makes more sense than anything else I've ever read.
▲ Page top
Reaver or Naginata?
QuoteReply
# Apr 23 2003 at 2:47 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591788439512
11 posts
I picked up a Worn Shai`din Naginata by trading my FBSS. Quite a deal I got cause this weapon is 37 damage to the reaver's 40, sure, it is slower than the reaver with a 45 attack delay compared to 40 of the reaver, but with the reaver costing 4k-7k on Rodcet Nife and my weapon was selling for 1k (cheaply, I might add) I think this weapon is a steal. It gets overlooked, the Naginata because nobody has heard of it but it is almost as good as the reaver, and considerably cheaper. If it's affordable I'll get the reaver but not after buying better armor, etc. Take a look at the Worn Shai`din Naginata, you may not regret it especially if cash is a problem or not quite into the 40's yet. Plus it's +6 str, +6 sta, +2 wis +2 int and 7 damage bonus. It's probably rare to find but a steal. Got it at level 32 and with 157 strength unbuffed am hitting for 130 damage tops.

Hifglar Stormblade
35 Paladin

Reason for change
QuoteReply
# Jan 11 2003 at 6:29 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591750583624
34 posts
Probably changed it because rogues were pick-pocketing it before :)

▲ Page top
RE: Reason for change
QuoteReply
# Mar 19 2003 at 4:15 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255011925
Scholar
45 posts
It being flagged as non-magical was probably just an oversight that went uncorrected for a long time. Considering who drops it, I don't think the weapon was ever intended to be non-magical. I just think whomever put the weapon info into the database messed up entering the info.
▲ Page top
RE: Reason for change
QuoteReply
# Jan 21 2003 at 5:25 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592250918598
View User Forum
Scholar
44 posts
Too many were finding their way to lowly level 20s. Since no "ubers" or highbys would be caught dead with a non-magical weapon, the non-magic Reaver were gotten rid off at fire sale prices (2K to 5K) at the bazaar. Lowbys that bought it at such prices realised that 90% of the content out there DON'T REQUIRE MAGIC WEAPONS. Thus, what was a neat idea (conditional high end weapon that could only be used on non-magic mobs) became a potential game-breaking weapon if too many of them flowed downwards. A "reverse nerf" occured, the weapon became magical, demand for the weapon has risen amongst the high end crowd and the prices have risen accordingly to 10K+ plats out of the hands of lowly (but poor) players.

All is well in the game now....
▲ Page top
RE: Reason for change
QuoteReply
# Jan 23 2003 at 4:49 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592250918598
View User Forum
Scholar
44 posts
oh BTW, I have have since learnt that Rogues CAN and DID pickpocket the old non-magic Reavers. THAT is also a good reason to make it Magical! ROFL.

like it

# Jan 06 2003 at 8:21 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1633306622282
429 posts
Besides for raids and the rare encounter of a magic mob, the average monster isn't magic. I have no problem hitting just about everything in kunark, odus, antonica, etc with it. None the less i still carry a cheap 2hs for the occaisional magic encounter, right now i'm using a weighty polearm, which is very nice considering i picked it up for 175pp. Not everyone is rich enough to get a windblade or widebladed greatsword(althought i'd still use this over greatsword, switch for magic stuff) and for the 4-6k it goes for on normal servers(non pvp) i think its quite a bargin!

View User Forum
133 posts
I've carried this wep for 3 lvls I'm now a lvl 51 barb warrior on Torv, I love this wep I hit with it in the high 150+ my highest crit is 277 and with an enc or shammy haste it rocks, I've rarely come across mob that cannot be hit with it, and on the rare occassions that I have I can swap to my lammy and LSOEE and hardly miss a swing.) almost every group I've been in asks me what the weapon is I'm using after the 1st couple of fights. That said I've been considering buying stonebladed axe of dismay, I bought the reaver for 8k a couple of months back, I must admit I was shocked when I did a bazaar search to compare my resale price of 7k to see 2 for sale one at 5k and one at 4k! With the fun and success I've had wielding this beast I'd rather destroy it than give such a good weapon away(well maybe not!)but considering what mob drops this and the difficulty in obtaining it, 5k and 4k is a joke!

Slace 51 Warrior

Zappitt 51 Wizard

Slacethehard 57 Shammy

# Oct 03 2002 at 2:33 PM Rating: Default

15 posts
Isn't it funny how it has awesome resists & yet...it's not a magic item. What a joke!

Anyway, great DPS, but I wouldn't spend any pp on a nonmagical weapon unless I knew I'd be making a huge profit on the resell.

If you wanna spend a ton of money on a 2hander, invest in Nerandi's Lance or Argent Protector (shd/pal). If you're a warrior go for a Savage Stone Axe or Stonebladed Axe of Dismay.

My 2cp.

Edited, Thu Oct 3 15:18:22 2002
▲ Page top
Thoughts on the Reaver
QuoteReply
# Sep 13 2002 at 10:29 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592254940873
View User Forum
11 posts
The damage to speed ratio is fantastic and the magic saves always welcome but I find after having used this, I prefer other weapons: The Black Acrylia Halberd is 40/48 but has a magic bonus of +4 so if a mob's magic resist fails the saving throw, it becomes a 44/48 weapon (and also lifetaps for 45 hp oaccassionally). While it is indeed slower than the Reaver (delay 40 vs. delay 48), this can be compensated by a haste buff (I don't however recommend soloing tougher mobs with the BAH without haste and stamina buffs etc. It is far too slow to be used at full delay). Additionally, my greatest regret in buying this weapon is that it is, in fact, non-magical. There has been some debate on this point, but I assure you, you cannot defeat a willowisp with it (and no Grim Aura does not imbue your weapon with magical properties). My final thought is on price...on many servers, this weapon ranges from 15-20k which I feel is grossly overpriced despite the great dmg/dly. You can buy the Black Acrylia Halberd for 10k (or under) and it will serve you well. The Reaver is a good weapon; but I don't feel it makes the grade for its worth.
~ Valyrae Darkmalice
Grave Lord on Xev
